authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(152) — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Tahoe Lake and tributaries)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(a)(1) — General Provisions (waters listed in subsection (b) closed except during the open season shown)
sources.quotedText: Every body of water listed in subsection (b) is closed to all fishing except during the open season as shown.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Dollar Creek not listed by name)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: List of Lake Tahoe inflow streams — Wikipedia (Dollar Creek listed under North shore)
sources.quotedText: Dollar Creek ... flows into Lake Tahoe at Dollar Point, California

sources.title: USGS FS-035-02 — Table 1. Information for USGS sites in the Lake Tahoe Basin, California and Nevada
sources.quotedText: 10336684 Dollar Creek near Tahoe City, Calif. 1973-74 1.07

groups.humanSummary: Dollar Creek is a north-shore Lake Tahoe tributary — it flows into Lake Tahoe at Dollar Point (Placer Co.; USGS lists it as a Lake Tahoe Basin stream, 'Dollar Creek near Tahoe City'). It is not listed under its own name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), but as a Lake Tahoe tributary it is governed by the special Tahoe-tributary regulation §7.50(b)(152)(A): catch-and-release open season Sat. before Memorial Day–Sep. 30, artificial lures with barbless hooks only, 0 trout; closed the remainder of the year per §7.50(a)(1). There is no lake on Dollar Creek's short (~1 sq mi drainage) main stem, so 'upstream to the first lake' covers the entire creek.
groups.verbatimText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

parameters.note: Dollar Creek is a tributary of Lake Tahoe (it flows into the lake at Dollar Point, north shore, Placer Co.). It is not listed by its own name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), but it falls under the Lake Tahoe tributaries entry §7.50(b)(152)(A) ('Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake'), NOT the general last-Sat-April–Nov-15 trout stream season. Per §7.50(a)(1), waters listed in subsection (b) are closed to all fishing except during the shown open season — so the creek is closed Oct. 1 through the Friday preceding the Saturday before Memorial Day. Date_spec encodes 'Saturday preceding Memorial Day' as the last Monday of May (Memorial Day) minus 2 days, since the resolver has no anchor_holiday implementation.

regulations.humanSummary: Open catch-and-release only, Saturday before Memorial Day through Sept. 30; closed to all fishing the rest of the year (Lake Tahoe tributary special regulation).
regulations.verbatimText: Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

regulations.humanSummary: 0 trout (catch-and-release) during the open catch-and-release window.
Artificial lures with barbless hooks only during the open window; no bait (gear restriction applies to all species per §7.50(a)(1)).
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Dollar Creek is wholly within California (Placer Co.); no Nevada reciprocity applies to the creek.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+.
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
